root_text.sn48.19:1.1: Atha kho aññataro bhikkhu yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho so bhikkhu bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn48.19:2.2: Kittāvatā nu kho, bhante, indriyasampanno hotī”ti?
root_text.sn48.19:2.3: “Idha, bhikkhu, bhikkhu saddhindriyaṁ bhāveti upasamagāmiṁ sambodhagāmiṁ, vīriyindriyaṁ bhāveti upasamagāmiṁ sambodhagāmiṁ, satindriyaṁ bhāveti upasamagāmiṁ sambodhagāmiṁ, samādhindriyaṁ bhāveti upasamagāmiṁ sambodhagāmiṁ, paññindriyaṁ bhāveti upasamagāmiṁ sambodhagāmiṁ.
root_text.sn48.19:2.4: Ettāvatā kho, bhikkhu, bhikkhu indriyasampanno hotī”ti.

translation_text.sn48.19:1.1: Then a mendicant went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and said to him:
translation_text.sn48.19:2.1: “Sir, they speak of someone who is ‘accomplished in the faculties’.
translation_text.sn48.19:2.2: How is someone accomplished in the faculties defined?”
translation_text.sn48.19:2.3: “Mendicant, it’s when a mendicant develops the faculties of faith, energy, mindfulness, immersion, and wisdom that lead to peace and awakening.
translation_text.sn48.19:2.4: This is how someone who is accomplished in the faculties is defined.”
